rendered_lines: (1) From 2017 onwards, financial information is presented on a financial year basis ending 31 December. Financial information for 2016 is presented on a financial year basis ending 30 November.

rendered_lines: (8) From 2019 onwards, ANP and VONB for Other Markets include our share of results attributable to our 49 per cent interest in Tata AIA Life Insurance Company Limited (Tata AIA Life). ANP and VONB reported for 2018 and prior years have not been restated and do not include any contribution from Tata AIA Life. From 2019 onwards, the Group's VONB excludes VONB attributable to non-controlling interests. VONB for 2018 and prior years has not been restated and, as previously disclosed, has not been adjusted for non-controlling interests. Tata AIA Life's International Financial Reporting Standards results are accounted for using the equity method of accounting. The Group's consolidated financial statements use Tata AIA Life financial information on a one quarter lag basis. For clarity, total weighted premium income does not include any contribution from Tata AIA Life.

rendered_by_block.p9_b32: (8) From 2019 onwards, ANP and VONB for Other Markets include our share of results attributable to our 49 per cent interest in Tata AIA Life Insurance Company Limited (Tata AIA Life). VONB for 2018 and prior years has not been restated and, as previously disclosed, has not been adjusted for non-controlling interests. Tata AIA Life's International Financial Reporting Standards results are accounted for using the equity method of accounting. The Group's consolidated financial statements use Tata AIA Life financial information on a one quarter lag basis. For clarity, total weighted premium income does not include any contribution from Tata AIA Life.

rendered_lines: (2) VONB is the present value, at point of sale, of projected after-tax statutory profits from new business sold during the period less the cost of required capital to support the new business in excess of statutory reserves.

rendered_by_block.p9_b33: (2) VONB is the present value, at point of sale, of projected after-tax statutory profits from new business sold during the period less the cost of required capital to support the new business in excess of statutory reserves.

rendered_lines: (3) ANP is a measure of new business volume, calculated as 100% of annualised first year premiums and 10% of single premiums, before reinsurance ceded.

rendered_by_block.p9_b34: (3) ANP is a measure of new business volume, calculated as 100% of annualised first year premiums and 10% of single premiums, before reinsurance ceded.

rendered_lines: (6) EV is an actuarial estimate of the economic value of life insurance business based on a specific set of assumptions about future experience, excluding the economic value of any future new business. EV Equity is the sum of EV, goodwill and other intangible assets.

rendered_lines: During the COVID-19 pandemic,
Our colleagues care for communities and fulfil duties

rendered_lines: Serving millions of customers
Uninterrupted Support

AIA has a long and distinguished history in Asia, earning a reputation for managing its business well through periods of social upheaval and uncertainty, demonstrating that AIA is trustworthy, resilient and committed to doing the right thing for its stakeholders. Since early 2020, the COVID-19 pandemic has resulted in a constantly changing and increasingly complex business environment, with unprecedented shifts in the political, macroeconomic and capital market landscape. In this context, AIA's financial strength and purpose of helping people live Healthier, Longer, Better Lives has taken on even greater significance. I would like to express my heartfelt gratitude to all of AIA's employees, agents and partners who, even in the face of the most severe challenges, have remained caring towards the community, dedicated to their duties, and focused on serving our customers,

VONB declined to US$2.765 billion as new business sales were impacted by COVID-19-related control measures. We rapidly adopted innovative digital tools to enhance operational efficiency, and coupled with the recent easing of movement restrictions, sales momentum has recovered strongly and continued into early 2021.

rendered_by_block.p10_b19: VONB declined to US$2.765 billion as new business sales were impacted by COVID-19-related control measures. We rapidly adopted innovative digital tools to enhance operational efficiency,

rendered_lines: 008 AIA Group Limited

In the fourth quarter of 2020, we celebrated the 10th anniversary of AIA's listing on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. Looking back, AIA's listing set multiple records and remains the largest initial public offering in the global insurance industry to date. It has been my privilege and pleasure to work alongside the members of the Board. During this period, AIA's business has continued to grow and expand, becoming the largest life insurance company in the world by market capitalisation.

rendered_by_block.p11_b1: In the fourth quarter of 2020, we celebrated the 10th anniversary of AIA's listing on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. Looking back, AIA's listing set multiple records and remains the largest initial public offering in the global insurance industry to date.

One of the Board's key responsibilities is to ensure a strong leadership pipeline and effective succession arrangements. We welcomed Mr. Lee Yuan Siong on 1 June 2020 as Group Chief Executive and President, succeeding Mr. Ng Keng Hooi who retired. On behalf of the Board, I would like to express our deep gratitude to Mr. Lee and his management team for their excellent leadership in navigating our business forward during this unprecedented environment. We also extend our heartfelt thanks to Mr. Ng Keng Hooi for

rendered_lines: Environmental, Social and Governance issues are becoming increasingly important globally. We are committed to doing everything we can to help drive society towards a better future, and our Environmental, Social and Governance initiatives were once again recognised in 2020. According to ratings agency Sustainalytics, AIA ranked second among more than 270 insurance companies, demonstrating our excellent corporate governance strength and quality. In addition, we continued to receive the 'Prime' ESG Corporate Rating from Institutional Shareholder Services Inc. (ISS), recognising us as one of the industry's best performers in sustainability. We believe there is still more we need to do in this area to achieve better outcomes for our communities. This remains a personal priority for me and the Board

rendered_lines: Today, customer demand for AIA's products, services and high-quality advice has never been more compelling. Our strategy is built on Asia's strong structural growth drivers and is supported by multiple factors, including unprecedented wealth creation, a rapidly widening protection gap, ongoing shifts in consumer preferences, the proliferation of digital technology, and market focus on long-term sustainable development. The Board is confident that AIA is exceptionally well positioned to capture the significant opportunities across the region, providing financial protection and peace of mind to customers and their families when they need it most, while creating shared value for all stakeholders.

rendered_lines: Since the onset of the pandemic, our business teams across the Group have responded swiftly and proactively to help alleviate the impact on communities while ensuring the safety of our employees. I am proud of the wide range of measures we have implemented, including financial support, provision of medical supplies, free coverage, access to healthcare resources, streamlined service processes and expedited claims payments. True to our commitment to finding every reason to pay, we paid over US$16 billion in insurance benefits and claims during the year, providing vital financial protection to our customers.

rendered_lines: Benefits and Claims

Despite measures to contain the spread of COVID-19 leading to a reduction in VONB to US$2.765 billion, sales momentum turned positive as restrictions eased, and 2021 began with very strong momentum, with VONB growing 15% year-on-year in the first two months. The high-quality and recurring income stream generated by our large and growing in-force portfolio drove a 5% increase in OPAT in 2020, while UFSG grew 7% during the period. rendered_lines: 2020 Performance Highlights

AIA China was the largest contributor to the Group's VONB in 2020 and has delivered an excellent start to 2021, with very strong VONB growth in the first two months of the year compared with the same period in 2020. While VONB of US$968 million in 2020 was 17% lower than in 2019, this was primarily due to limited new sales in the initial stages of the COVID-19 outbreak in the first quarter. rendered_lines: In 2020, we successfully obtained regulatory approval to convert our Shanghai branch into Mainland China's first wholly foreign-owned life insurance subsidiary. In March 2021, we received approval from the China Banking and Insurance Regulatory Commission to commence operations in Sichuan Province. Sichuan Province is the sixth largest province in Mainland China by GDP and the fourth largest by population, presenting significant long-term growth opportunities for AIA.

rendered_lines: Hong Kong implemented travel restrictions in early February 2020, and new business sales from Mainland Chinese visitors effectively came to a halt, resulting in a significant decline in VONB. Compared to the first half of the year, our agency distribution business from local customers recorded double-digit sales growth in the second half, maintaining a significant market leadership position. VONB from the partnership distribution channel was significantly lower than in 2019, due to the historically higher proportion of sales to Mainland Chinese visitors through the retail independent financial adviser channel. OPAT increased by 10%, benefiting from strong growth in renewal premiums and favourable claims experience.

People-Centric
AIA's 23,000 employees are a key competitive advantage for the Group and critical to our continued success. We promote an inclusive and collaborative

rendered_lines: learning culture, providing our employees with a platform to develop meaningful and rewarding careers at AIA. In response to social distancing measures and travel restrictions, we migrated our learning content online to the 'AIA Learning Hub'. In addition, AIA was included in Forbes' 'World's Best Employers 2020' list.

rendered_lines: The core components of our 'Organisation of the Future' strategy include strengthening and simplifying our organisational structure, introducing flexible working arrangements, and enhancing our capability framework. These strategic priorities will support our employees' career development and further strengthen AIA's position as an employer of choice.

rendered_lines: Since the second half of 2020, we have embarked on a journey to shape a transformational organisation through two pilot business units – AIA's operations in Singapore and Vietnam. Initial results have delivered a more streamlined, customer-centric operating model, and we have also implemented flexible working arrangements and established multiple cross-functional expert teams. We plan to experiment and learn within these pilot businesses before expanding the Organisation of the Future initiative to other operations.

rendered_lines: ESG

For over a century, AIA has made a positive and lasting impact on the lives of our customers across Asia. Our communities face a number of environmental, social and governance issues, including climate change, environmental pollution and lifestyle-related diseases. The ongoing pandemic has highlighted the complexity of these challenges. As a lifelong partner to our customers and communities, we are doing everything we can to drive society towards a better and more sustainable future.

rendered_lines: AIA participates in the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures, a global organisation dedicated to establishing a voluntary and consistent disclosure framework on climate change impacts. We continue to reaffirm our commitment to its recommendations, engaging with over 1,400 companies in our investment portfolio and more than 30 external investment managers in 2020 to influence how they manage climate change risks and address climate issues.

rendered_lines: Outlook

In 2020, the COVID-19 pandemic was undoubtedly the largest public health emergency in living memory. Unprecedented public fiscal and financial support provided to affected businesses and households helped mitigate some of the extreme financial shocks caused by the pandemic and accelerated the pace of recovery, particularly in countries with robust health policies and infrastructure. However, significant disparities existed between countries, and under these circumstances, Asia (excluding Japan) recorded the relatively strongest GDP performance globally. At present, the global economy is just beginning to emerge from a deep recession and enter the initial stages of recovery.

rendered_by_block.p16_b7: In 2020, the COVID-19 pandemic was undoubtedly the largest public health emergency in living memory. Unprecedented public fiscal and financial support provided to affected businesses
rendered_by_block.p16_b8: and households helped mitigate some of the extreme financial shocks caused by the pandemic and accelerated the pace of recovery, particularly in countries with robust health policies and infrastructure.

rendered_lines: para.

While the impact of the pandemic is not yet over, we remain cautiously optimistic that 2021 will be a transitional year. Markets are encouraged by the rollout of vaccination programmes and new therapies that are expected to help reduce infection rates and severity, enabling healthcare systems to respond more effectively to the pandemic and lower mortality rates. Although we do not expect travel or consumer behaviour to return to pre-pandemic levels in the near term, as consumer confidence recovers, we expect the scope of recovery to gradually expand from manufacturing-led industries to consumption and services sectors. This will be particularly evident in countries with younger populations.

rendered_lines: I am confident about AIA's long-term future prospects. Despite near-term uncertainties, strong domestic demand drivers, combined with Asia's major demographic trends, continue to provide positive structural support for the long-term outlook of AIA's business. With rising household incomes, low private insurance penetration, and limited social welfare coverage, demand for AIA's products will continue to grow. We will leverage our significant competitive advantages and strong growth track record to build a more sustainable future for our communities and create shared value for all stakeholders.

rendered_lines: Financial Summary

In 2020, AIA delivered strong financial performance amid the unprecedented business and macroeconomic conditions brought about by the global pandemic. Although movement restrictions led to a decline in VONB in 2020, we saw a recovery in sales momentum as the business adapted and restrictions eased. This momentum has continued into 2021, with VONB in the first two months growing 15% compared to the same period in 2020. Our prudent financial management over the years and consistent strategic focus on high-quality business are reflected in the sustained growth of OPAT and UFSG, a robust and resilient capital position, and dividends per share

rendered_lines: 016 AIA Group Limited

The primary impact of the pandemic on our 2020 financial performance was on new business sales, as containment measures in many of our markets restricted face-to-face sales activities, particularly in the first half of 2020. Although VONB of US$2.765 billion in 2020 decreased by 33% compared to 2019, we rapidly adopted new digital tools, and the widespread easing of movement restrictions supported a recovery in sales momentum. AIA China made the largest contribution to the Group's VONB in 2020 and was our first reporting segment to show a recovery in sales momentum, as the region progressively eased strict lockdowns from March 2020 onwards. Excluding sales to Mainland Chinese visitors to Hong Kong, which remained at low levels, VONB in the rest of the Group's segments rebounded strongly in the second half, growing 23% compared to the first half.

rendered_lines: This was driven by EV operating profit of US$7.243 billion, which offset the impact of lower government bond yields. EV operating profit included US$549 million from positive operating variances, reflecting our continued positive overall experience relative to our EV assumptions.

rendered_by_block.p19_b10: This was driven by EV operating profit of US$7.243 billion, which offset the impact of lower government bond yields. EV operating profit included US$549 million from positive operating variances,

rendered_lines: Our high-quality, recurring earnings sources and active management of our in-force portfolio drove OPAT growth of 5% to US$5.942 billion and maintained a stable operating margin of 16.9%. Renewal premiums received increased by 10%, with total recurring premiums accounting for over 90% of premiums received in 2020. OPAT growth benefited from significantly positive claims experience throughout the year, which offset the impact of lower yields on new fixed income investments and the assumed reduction in long-term equity investment returns. As expected, the exceptionally positive health claims experience reported in the first half did not recur in the second half, but we continued to see a positive contribution from this source to OPAT growth. Persistency improved in the second half as lapse experience in AIA Thailand normalized, with Group persistency at 95%.

rendered_lines: UFSG of US$5.843 billion increased by 7%, driven by the continued growth of our in-force portfolio and our active management of it, which was sufficient to offset the impact of lower expected investment returns. As at 31 December 2020, the Group's financial position remained very strong, with Free Surplus of US$13.473 billion after payment of dividends of US$1.997 billion.

rendered_lines: As at 31 December 2020, the solvency ratio of our principal operating company, AIA Company Limited (AIA Co.), remained very strong at 489%. As at 31 December 2020, under the new group regulatory framework, the Group LCSM cover ratio was 374%. Going forward, we will disclose the Group LCSM cover ratio as the primary measure of the Group's regulatory solvency.

rendered_lines: The Board has proposed a final dividend of 100.30 Hong Kong cents per share, an increase of 7.5%, subject to shareholders' approval at the Company's forthcoming Annual General Meeting. This brings the total dividend for 2020 to 135.30 Hong Kong cents per share, an increase of 6.9% over the total dividend for 2019. This is in line with AIA's long-standing prudent, sustainable and progressive dividend policy, which allows the Group to capture future growth opportunities while maintaining financial flexibility.

rendered_lines: We remain confident in the business opportunities for AIA across the region. We will continue to focus on investing capital to deliver profitable new business growth and leverage our competitive advantages, while maintaining prudent financial management and delivering long-term shareholder value.

rendered_lines: Although VONB of US$2.765 billion in 2020 decreased by 33% compared to 2019, we rapidly adopted new digital tools and the widespread easing of mobility restrictions supported a recovery in sales momentum. AIA China was the largest contributor to the Group's VONB in 2020 and was our first reporting segment to show a recovery in sales momentum, as the region progressively eased strict lockdowns from March 2020. Excluding Mainland Chinese visitor sales in Hong Kong, which remained low, VONB in the rest of the Group rebounded strongly in the second half of the year, growing 23% compared to the first half. The Group's new business momentum continued to be strong in 2021, with VONB in the first two months 15% higher than the same period in 2020.

rendered_by_block.p20_b21: Although VONB of US$2.765 billion in 2020 decreased by 33% compared to 2019, we rapidly adopted new digital tools and the widespread easing of mobility restrictions supported a recovery in sales momentum.

rendered_lines: AIA China recorded successful momentum in 2021, with VONB in the first two months showing very strong growth compared to the same period in 2020. VONB of US$968 million in 2020 decreased by 17% from 2019, primarily due to limited sales during the initial outbreak of COVID-19 in the first quarter. As movement restrictions eased, new business momentum improved rapidly. VONB resumed our customary seasonal pattern, with greater weighting towards the first half of the year. In 2020, AIA China was the largest contributor to the Group's VONB.

rendered_by_block.p20_b26: AIA China recorded successful momentum in 2021, with VONB in the first two months showing very strong growth compared to the same period in 2020.

rendered_by_block.p20_b29: In 2020, AIA China was the largest contributor to the Group's VONB.

rendered_lines: Following the implementation of travel restrictions, new business sales from Mainland Chinese visitors to AIA Hong Kong have been effectively suspended since early February 2020, resulting in a significant decline in VONB. The VONB margin recorded a decrease, reflecting the impact of underwriting expense overruns due to reduced new business volumes, changes in product mix and lower interest rates. Macau resumed the Individual Visit Scheme for Mainland Chinese travelers at the end of September 2020. This supported a recovery in sales to Mainland Chinese visitors to Macau, which accounted for over one-third of total ANP at AIA Macau in the fourth quarter.
